description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves create a striped pattern, alternating between the rough, speckled stone and deep black channels. The stone appears to be a type of granite or similar hard rock, with a mottled gray and greenish coloration. The block is solid and robust, with clean, sharp edges and a uniform thickness. Its sides are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. This design suggests it is intended for use as a tactile or anti-slip surface in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a barrier system.
description: A rectangular granite block with parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped, anti-slip pattern. The stone is speckled gray-green with deep black channels and smooth sides.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved side, showing the alternating stone and black channels running horizontally.
description_view_1: The grooves are viewed lengthwise, emphasizing the depth and regular spacing of the black channels.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the horizontal grooves dominate, but the perspective is slightly shifted.
description_view_3: The grooves are again viewed lengthwise, with the striped pattern appearing more pronounced due to the angle.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a tactile or anti-slip barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with sharp grooves.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous surface or barrier with adjacent blocks
